title: Daily Briefing — Mar 18
headline: Extreme fear, mean reversion in play, but don't get greedy on the first bounce.
body: Right, so we've got Fear & Greed at 25 — that's officially extreme fear territory. SPY, QQQ, and most tech leaders are all below their 20 MA, which is a big warning sign. Volatility is still elevated (VIX 21.55 under its 20 SMA), but it's starting to contract — classic setup for a mean reversion rally, but patience is key.

Sector rotation is a mixed bag: Energy (XLE) and Materials (XLB) are weakening, while Discretionary (XLY) and Tech (XLK) are showing early signs of improvement on the RRG, but still under key MAs. Financials (XLF) are lagging badly below all major averages (SMA20: 50.86, last: 49.56) and showing elevated risk — remember, the canary in the coal mine.

Contrarian lens: We are at a zone where panic is high, but institutions aren't stepping in with conviction yet (no major dark pool signals, no put/call washout). Don't chase green days here — abundance mindset. Wait for a decisive reclaim of the 20 MA on leaders or a proper flush before sizing up.
callout_body:
SPY: Watch 680.19 (SMA20) and 657.27 (SMA200) — mean reversion possible, but rejection likely under 680
QQQ: 605.38 (SMA20) is resistance — need a daily close above to flip bias
XLF: Below all key MAs — risk stays elevated as long as under 50.86 (SMA20)
Fear & Greed: 25 — extreme fear (per playbook: start building watchlist, don't FOMO in early)
Energy (XLE): Still strong on relative strength, but momentum rolling over
Tech (XLK): 139.54 (SMA20) is the pivot — rejection so far
action_items: Watch for SPY/QQQ daily closes back above 20 MA before considering new longs — no trigger yet.
Defensive until Financials (XLF) can reclaim 50.86+; avoid chasing Energy or Tech strength on day one of a bounce.
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price. Patience, react, don't predict.
